title: Greenland Glacier-Mining Nexus Explorer
text:
This project develops an interactive Google Earth Engine application to explore glacier retreat susceptibility across Greenland’s peripheral glacier zone. The app combines Landsat-derived glacier change, Landsat land surface temperature trends, ArcticDEM terrain variables, and proximity indicators for mining activity, settlements, and coastlines. A Random Forest model is trained on observed retreat and stable-ice labels to map where current glaciers are more susceptible to retreat.
The purpose is not to prove that mining caused glacier retreat. Instead, the application provides a screening tool for identifying places where observed retreat, climate warming, terrain conditions, and mining-related proximity overlap. This supports more transparent discussion around Arctic resource development, environmental monitoring, and glacier vulnerability.


Greenland’s peripheral glaciers are changing rapidly under Arctic warming, while renewed mineral exploration creates planning questions in sensitive cryospheric environments. Existing glacier products and mining datasets are usually viewed separately, making it difficult for non-specialist users to compare climate-driven change with human activity indicators.
This project addresses that gap by building a web-based decision-support tool that:

maps glacier extent change from approximately 2000 to 2024;
estimates summer land surface temperature trends;
integrates elevation, slope, mining proximity, settlement proximity, and coastal proximity;
predicts retreat susceptibility using a validated Random Forest model;
communicates model outputs through an interactive split-map interface.




The application is designed for users who need a clear spatial overview rather than a static scientific map. Potential users include Arctic environmental researchers, Greenland planning and resource governance agencies, environmental NGOs, mining impact analysts, and students studying remote sensing or climate risk.
The interface supports these users by combining technical model outputs with accessible interaction: users can compare historical and recent glacier extent, toggle model layers, inspect model diagnostics, and click anywhere on the map to retrieve local glacier, temperature, elevation, and susceptibility statistics.






Category
Dataset
Use in the project
Source




Satellite imagery
Landsat 7/8/9 Collection 2 Level-2
Summer NDSI glacier extent and land surface temperature trends from ca. 2000 to 2024
USGS Landsat Collection 2


Glacier mask context
GIMP 2000 Ice/Ocean Mask
Land-ice context and model-domain masking
Google Earth Engine GIMP catalog


Terrain
ArcticDEM V4.1 2 m mosaic
Elevation and slope predictors, sampled to the modelling scale
Google Earth Engine ArcticDEM catalog


Boundaries
LSIB Simple 2017
Greenland boundary and analysis region
Google Earth Engine LSIB catalog


Mining activity
Active mineral licence polygons and post-2000 drillhole points
Mining proximity predictor and map overlay
Greenland Mineral Resources Portal / project uploads


Settlements
Greenland settlements and cities
Human-accessibility proximity predictor and map overlay
Project uploads


Reference grid
15-degree graticules
Polar map orientation and visual reference
Project upload

title: Greenland Glacier-Mining Nexus Explorer
Greenland Glacier-Mining Nexus Explorer
text:
The Application
Replace the placeholder below with the published Earth Engine App link before final submission.

title: Greenland Glacier-Mining Nexus Explorer
Greenland Glacier-Mining Nexus Explorer
text:
References

CASA25 Rasternauts. Nepal Landslides Risk Application. Used as a website-structure reference for the “How it Works” section. https://maheer-maps.github.io/CASA25_Rasternauts/
Breiman, L. (2001). Random Forests. Machine Learning, 45, 5-32. https://doi.org/10.1023/A:1010933404324
GEUS. Greenland Portal. Used as the institutional basis for Greenland mineral-resource and licence data context. https://eng.geus.dk/products-services-facilities/data-and-maps/greenland-portal
Google Earth Engine Data Catalog. ArcticDEM Mosaic V4.1. Used for elevation and slope predictors. https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/UMN_PGC_ArcticDEM_V4_2m_mosaic
Google Earth Engine Data Catalog. GIMP Greenland Ice and Ocean Mask. Used for land-ice context and model-domain masking. https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/OSU_GIMP_2000_ICE_OCEAN_MASK
Roberts, D. R. et al. (2017). Cross-validation strategies for data with temporal, spatial, hierarchical, or phylogenetic structure. Ecography, 40(8), 913-929. https://doi.org/10.1111/ecog.02881
USGS. Landsat Collection 2 Surface Temperature. Used for LST scaling and summer temperature-trend analysis. https://www.usgs.gov/landsat-missions/landsat-collection-2-surface-temperature
USGS. Normalized Difference Snow Index. Used for the NDSI snow/ice mapping formula and Landsat band choices. https://www.usgs.gov/landsat-missions/normalized-difference-snow-index
USGS. Landsat Shapefiles and KML Files. Used to justify caution around routine WRS-2 coverage near very high latitudes. https://www.usgs.gov/landsat-missions/landsat-shapefiles-and-kml-files
